message.content: Write a README for this project. It's a desktop control panel for your claude code sessions. It lets you index, search, and back up old claude code sessions so that you can always revisit and access them. Also say that because it uses Entire.io, it links your AI sessions with your commits so that you never lose the context behind your code. Include instructions on how to run the app locally. Mention that you need the entire cli installed, and that it's an electron app with a server and ui component. Also say that brew installs are coming soon. Make it exciting

content.content:
app
dist
node_modules
package-lock.json
package.json
prisma
prisma.config.ts
src
tsconfig.json
{
  "name": "claude-hooks-listener",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Gossamer: indexes Entire CLI logs and serves them via HTTP + WebSocket",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": {
    "gossamer-serve": "./dist/serve.js"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "prisma generate && tsc",
    "serve": "node dist/serve.js",
    "start": "concurrently --names \"serve,app\" -c \"blue,green\" \"npm run serve\" \"cd app && npm run dev\"",
    "db:push": "DATABASE_URL=\"file:$HOME/.claude/hook-handler.db\" prisma db push",
    "db:studio": "prisma studio"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3": "^7.4.2",
    "@prisma/client": "^7.4.2",
    "better-sqlite3": "^12.6.2",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "express": "^4.18.2",
    "gitlog": "^5.1.0",
    "ws": "^8.18.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.13",
    "@types/cors": "^2.8.17",
    "@types/express": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/ws": "^8.5.13",
    "concurrently": "^9.2.1",
    "prisma": "^7.4.2",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0"
  }
}

input.content:
# Gossamer

**Never lose the context behind your code again.**

Gossamer is a desktop control panel for your Claude Code sessions. It indexes, searches, and backs up every AI session you've ever run — so the thinking, the decisions, the rabbit holes, the breakthroughs — all of it is preserved and searchable forever.

And because Gossamer integrates with **[Entire.io](https://entire.io)**, your AI sessions are linked directly to your Git commits. Every line of code has a story. Now you can actually read it.

---

## What it does

- **Index** every Claude Code session automatically as you work
- **Search** across all your sessions — find that conversation where you figured out the auth bug three months ago
- **Back up** sessions so nothing is ever lost, even across machines
- **Link sessions to commits** via Entire.io — click any commit and see the full AI conversation behind it
- **Browse checkpoints** — step through the evolution of a session with diffs and context at every turn
- **Resume sessions** — jump back into past conversations right where you left off

---

## Requirements

- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v18+
- [Entire CLI](https://entire.io) installed and authenticated
- Claude Code installed and configured

> **Homebrew installs coming soon.** For now, clone and run locally.

---

## Getting started

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
cd gossamer
```

### 2. Install server dependencies and build

```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

### 3. Push the database schema

```bash
npm run db:push
```

This creates the SQLite database at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db`.

### 4. Install app dependencies

```bash
cd app && npm install && cd ..
```

### 5. Run

```bash
npm start
```

This launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together. The server indexes your sessions and exposes a local API; the Electron app is the UI that talks to it.

---

## Architecture

Gossamer has two components that run together:

| Component | What it does |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Server** (`src/serve.ts`) | Node.js + Express + WebSocket server. Indexes Claude Code sessions from disk, syncs with Entire.io, and serves a real-time API on `localhost:3000`. |
| **App** (`app/`) | Electron desktop app built with React + Mantine. Connects to the local server and renders your session history, search, checkpoints, and diffs. |

Both start with `npm start`.

---

## How the Entire.io integration works

Gossamer uses the Entire CLI to associate your Claude Code sessions with the Git commits they produced. When you make a commit while Claude Code is active, Entire captures the link between the session and the commit hash.

From Gossamer you can:
- See which session produced which commit
- Open a commit and read the full AI conversation that led to it
- Never wonder "why did we write it this way?" again

Make sure the Entire CLI is installed and you're logged in before starting Gossamer.

---

## Development

To work on the server and app separately:

```bash
# Server only (hot-reloads with tsc --watch)
npm run serve

# App only (Electron + Vite HMR)
cd app && npm run dev
```

Database UI:

```bash
npm run db:studio
```

---

## Coming soon

- `brew install gossamer` — one-line install, no Node required
- Cloud backup and sync across machines
- Team sharing — share session history with your whole org
- Native search with full-text indexing

---

Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.

The session history is stored in your local git repository and can be pushed to your remote repo. The data is completely under your control.

file.content:
# Gossamer

**Never lose the context behind your code again.**

Gossamer is a desktop control panel for your Claude Code sessions. It indexes, searches, and backs up every AI session you've ever run — so the thinking, the decisions, the rabbit holes, the breakthroughs — all of it is preserved and searchable forever.

And because Gossamer integrates with **[Entire.io](https://entire.io)**, your AI sessions are linked directly to your Git commits. Every line of code has a story. Now you can actually read it.

---

## What it does

- **Index** every Claude Code session automatically as you work
- **Search** across all your sessions — find that conversation where you figured out the auth bug three months ago
- **Back up** sessions so nothing is ever lost, even across machines
- **Link sessions to commits** via Entire.io — click any commit and see the full AI conversation behind it
- **Browse checkpoints** — step through the evolution of a session with diffs and context at every turn
- **Resume sessions** — jump back into past conversations right where you left off

---

## Requirements

- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v18+
- [Entire CLI](https://entire.io) installed and authenticated
- Claude Code installed and configured

> **Homebrew installs coming soon.** For now, clone and run locally.

---

## Getting started

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
cd gossamer
```

### 2. Install server dependencies and build

```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

### 3. Push the database schema

```bash
npm run db:push
```

This creates the SQLite database at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db`.

### 4. Install app dependencies

```bash
cd app && npm install && cd ..
```

### 5. Run

```bash
npm start
```

This launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together. The server indexes your sessions and exposes a local API; the Electron app is the UI that talks to it.

---

## Architecture

Gossamer has two components that run together:

| Component | What it does |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Server** (`src/serve.ts`) | Node.js + Express + WebSocket server. Indexes Claude Code sessions from disk, syncs with Entire.io, and serves a real-time API on `localhost:3000`. |
| **App** (`app/`) | Electron desktop app built with React + Mantine. Connects to the local server and renders your session history, search, checkpoints, and diffs. |

Both start with `npm start`.

---

## How the Entire.io integration works

Gossamer uses the Entire CLI to associate your Claude Code sessions with the Git commits they produced. When you make a commit while Claude Code is active, Entire captures the link between the session and the commit hash.

From Gossamer you can:
- See which session produced which commit
- Open a commit and read the full AI conversation that led to it
- Never wonder "why did we write it this way?" again

Make sure the Entire CLI is installed and you're logged in before starting Gossamer.

---

## Development

To work on the server and app separately:

```bash
# Server only (hot-reloads with tsc --watch)
npm run serve

# App only (Electron + Vite HMR)
cd app && npm run dev
```

Database UI:

```bash
npm run db:studio
```

---

## Coming soon

- `brew install gossamer` — one-line install, no Node required
- Cloud backup and sync across machines
- Team sharing — share session history with your whole org
- Native search with full-text indexing

---

Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.

input.new_string:
## Your data, your machine

Gossamer is built on a simple principle: your AI sessions are yours. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no third-party storage. Everything stays local by default.

- **Search index and database** are stored in a single SQLite file at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db` — on your machine, readable with any SQLite tool, deletable whenever you want.
- **Session history** is stored in your local Git repository. Push it to your own remote to back it up or share it across machines — on your terms, using infrastructure you already control.
- **The server is local.** Gossamer's backend runs on `localhost` and never opens an external connection. The Electron app talks only to that local server.

You decide what gets backed up, who has access, and when it gets deleted. Gossamer never touches your data without you.

---

## Development

- **The server is local.** Gossamer's backend runs on `localhost` and never opens an external connection. The Electron app talks only to that local server.

You decide what gets backed up, who has access, and when it gets deleted. Gossamer never touches your data without you.

---

## Development

- **Search** across all your sessions — find that conversation where you figured out the auth bug three months ago
- **Back up** sessions so nothing is ever lost, even across machines
- **Link sessions to commits** via Entire.io — click any commit and see the full AI conversation behind it
- **Browse checkpoints** — step through the evolution of a session with diffs and context at every turn
- **Resume sessions** — jump back into past conversations right where you left off

---

## Requirements

- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v18+
- [Entire CLI](https://entire.io) installed and authenticated
- Claude Code installed and configured

> **Homebrew installs coming soon.** For now, clone and run locally.

---

## Getting started

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
cd gossamer
```

### 2. Install server dependencies and build

```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

### 3. Push the database schema

```bash
npm run db:push
```

This creates the SQLite database at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db`.

### 4. Install app dependencies

```bash
cd app && npm install && cd ..
```

### 5. Run

```bash
npm start
```

This launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together. The server indexes your sessions and exposes a local API; the Electron app is the UI that talks to it.

---

## Architecture

Gossamer has two components that run together:

| Component | What it does |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Server** (`src/serve.ts`) | Node.js + Express + WebSocket server. Indexes Claude Code sessions from disk, syncs with Entire.io, and serves a real-time API on `localhost:3000`. |
| **App** (`app/`) | Electron desktop app built with React + Mantine. Connects to the local server and renders your session history, search, checkpoints, and diffs. |

Both start with `npm start`.

---

## How the Entire.io integration works

Gossamer uses the Entire CLI to associate your Claude Code sessions with the Git commits they produced. When you make a commit while Claude Code is active, Entire captures the link between the session and the commit hash.

From Gossamer you can:
- See which session produced which commit
- Open a commit and read the full AI conversation that led to it
- Never wonder "why did we write it this way?" again

Make sure the Entire CLI is installed and you're logged in before starting Gossamer.

---

## Your data, your machine

Gossamer is built on a simple principle: your AI sessions are yours. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no third-party storage. Everything stays local by default.

- **Search index and database** are stored in a single SQLite file at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db` — on your machine, readable with any SQLite tool, deletable whenever you want.
- **Session history** is stored in your local Git repository. Push it to your own remote to back it up or share it across machines — on your terms, using infrastructure you already control.
- **The server is local.** Gossamer's backend runs on `localhost` and never opens an external connection. The Electron app talks only to that local server.

You decide what gets backed up, who has access, and when it gets deleted. Gossamer never touches your data without you.

---

## Development

To work on the server and app separately:

```bash
# Server only (hot-reloads with tsc --watch)
npm run serve

# App only (Electron + Vite HMR)
cd app && npm run dev
```

Database UI:

```bash
npm run db:studio
```

---

## Coming soon

- `brew install gossamer` — one-line install, no Node required
- Cloud backup and sync across machines
- Team sharing — share session history with your whole org
- Native search with full-text indexing

---

Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.

-- Cloud backup and sync across machines
- Team sharing — share session history with your whole org
